macro_segments.title: Introduction to the New Perek and the Use of Communal Funds for Public Sacrifices

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and transition to Daf 10, Perek on fund usage
What is done with the Terumah: Categories of Korbanot Tzibur purchased from communal funds
Non-animal sacrifices: Omer, Shtei HaLechem, Lechem HaPanim as communal sacrifices

macro_segments.title: The Omer on Shemita: Hiring Watchers and the Principle of Tzorchei Korban

micro_segments.title: Payment of watchers of wild growth during Shemita year from communal funds
Rabbi Yosi's opinion: unpaid volunteer watchers and donation of grain
Principle of Tzorchei Korban K'Korban: paying for needs enabling sacrifice
The Sages' objection: requirement that communal sacrifices come from communal funds

macro_segments.title: The Debate on Private Donations for Communal Purposes: Omer and Wood Donations

micro_segments.title: The Gemara's discussion of why private donations are questioned
The eight families and their wood donations to the Beit HaMikdash
The historical account: Return from Galut, wood shortage, and conditional donations
Rabbi Yacha D'Rabbi Yose: This follows Rabbi Yosi's position on private donations
The distinction: Private donations permitted for attendant matters, not for the Korban itself

macro_segments.title: Private Donations for Non-Sacrificial Items: White Tunic for Kohen Gadol

micro_segments.title: Woman weaving white tunic for Kohen Gadol on Yom Kippur
Rabbi Yosi position: Tunic can be donated; everyone agrees on non-Korban items
The Gemara proving Chachamim accept donations for wood despite earlier debate
The eight days as family holidays even post-Beit HaMikdash

macro_segments.title: Sources and Geography: Rabbi Yishmael's Position on the Omer's Origin

micro_segments.title: Doubling back to Omer and Shtei HaLechem from Eretz Yisrael only
Ten levels of holiness: the sanctity demonstrated by what grows in Eretz Yisrael
Rabbi Yishmael's view: Omer cannot come from Syria
Gemara discussion: how Rabbi Yishmael's reasoning connects cutting to mitzvah status
The question of planting grain for Omer on Shemita

macro_segments.title: Transferring Sanctity: Paying Labor from Sanctified Funds via the Banker

micro_segments.title: The mechanism: Banker pays workers, sanctified funds transfer to the object
Retroactive valuation: the paid amount becomes the assessed value of the grain
Application to stone carvers for Beit HaMikdash construction

macro_segments.title: What Korbanot Tzibur Can Be Purchased: Parah, Se'ir HaMishtaleach, and Attendant Items

micro_segments.title: Parah Adumah and Se'ir HaMishtaleach purchased from communal funds
Crimson strand and items used in the Parah Adumah ritual
Ramps for Parah and Se'ir HaMishtaleach to prevent tumah
City infrastructure: water channels, walls, and towers funded from Sheyarei HaLishka

macro_segments.title: Leftover Sheyarei HaLishka: What to Do with Desanctified Communal Funds

micro_segments.title: Abba Shaul: Kohen Gadol's private funding of Parah ramp
What to do with remaining Sheyarei HaLishka: buying wine, oil, flour for sale
Rabbi Yishmael: permitted to invest leftover funds
Rabbi Akiva: inappropriate for Hekdesh to conduct business
Extension: same principle applies to collected funds for the poor

macro_segments.title: Leftover Terumah: Gold Plating, Korban Kayitz, and Vessels

micro_segments.title: Gold plating for Kodesh HaKodashim walls as use for leftover Terumah

Rabbi Akiva: leftover Terumah for Kayitz HaMizbe'ach, not vessels
Leftover Nesachim and Rabbi Chananya: wine/flour offerings to Kayitz

macro_segments.title: Three Types of Strings and Their Weights: Preparation of Ritual Items

micro_segments.title: Three strings: Se'ir HaMishtaleach, Metzora, and Parah Adumah with different weights
Reasons for different weights: burning, division, and use in rituals

macro_segments.title: Expanded Uses of Terumah HaLishka: Paying Sages, Inspectors, and Scribes

micro_segments.title: Sages teaching Kohanim laws of slaughter, reception, and sprinkling
Inspectors of animal blemishes paid from communal funds
Correctors of the Sefer HaAzara and textual authorities
Two judges handling theft cases within the Beit HaMikdash

macro_segments.title: Women Weavers of the Parochet and the Debate over Fund Sources

micro_segments.title: Women weaving the Parochet: payment from Terumah or Bedek HaBayit
Shmuel: Parochet treated as Korban-like; Rav Chuna: Parochet as building structure
Resolution: Retroactive desanctification of funds by Beit Din for non-Korban uses
